## plugin:compound-engineering:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: macos-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Check out code
      uses: actions/checkout@v6

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v6
      with:
        go-version: '1.25.4'
        cache: true

    - name: Download dependencies
      run: go mod download

    - name: Verify dependencies
      run: go mod verify

    - name: Run go vet
      run: go vet ./...

    - name: Run gofmt
      run: |
        if [ "$(gofmt -s -l . | wc -l)" -gt 0 ]; then
          echo "The following files are not formatted:"
          gofmt -s -l .
          exit 1
        fi

    - name: Run tests
      run: make test

    - name: Run tests with race detector
      run: go test -race -v ./...

  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: macos-latest
    needs: test
    
    steps:
    - name: Check out code
      uses: actions/checkout@v6

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v6
      with:
        go-version: '1.25.4'
        cache: true

    - name: Build for Intel Mac
      run: GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o rekap-darwin-amd64 ./cmd/rekap

    - name: Build for Apple Silicon
      run: GOARCH=arm64 go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o rekap-darwin-arm64 ./cmd/rekap

    - name: Upload Intel binary
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v6
      with:
        name: rekap-darwin-amd64
        path: rekap-darwin-amd64
        retention-days: 30

    - name: Upload ARM binary
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v6
      with:
        name: rekap-darwin-arm64
        path: rekap-darwin-arm64
        retention-days: 30

  # lint:

on:

jobs:
  goreleaser:

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: macos-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Check out code
      uses: actions/checkout@v6

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v6
      with:
        go-version: '1.25.4'
        cache: true

    - name: Download dependencies
      run: go mod download

    - name: Verify dependencies
      run: go mod verify

    - name: Run go vet
      run: go vet ./...

    - name: Run gofmt
      run: |
        if [ "$(gofmt -s -l . | wc -l)" -gt 0 ]; then
          echo "The following files are not formatted:"
          gofmt -s -l .
          exit 1
        fi

    - name: Run tests
      run: make test

    - name: Run tests with race detector
      run: go test -race -v ./...

  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: macos-latest
    needs: test
    
    steps:
    - name: Check out code
      uses: actions/checkout@v6

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v6
      with:
        go-version: '1.25.4'
        cache: true

    - name: Build for Intel Mac
      run: GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o rekap-darwin-amd64 ./cmd/rekap

    - name: Build for Apple Silicon
      run: GOARCH=arm64 go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o rekap-darwin-arm64 ./cmd/rekap

    - name: Upload Intel binary
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v6
      with:
        name: rekap-darwin-amd64
        path: rekap-darwin-amd64
        retention-days: 30

    - name: Upload ARM binary
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v6
      with:
        name: rekap-darwin-arm64
        path: rekap-darwin-arm64
        retention-days: 30

38			AppSwitchesPerHour: 50,
39			MaxTabs:            100,
40			LateNightHour:      0,
41			NoBreakHours:       4,
42		}
43	}
44	
45	// CollectBurnout analyzes activity patterns for burnout indicators
46	func CollectBurnout(ctx context.Context, screen ScreenResult, browsers BrowsersResult, config BurnoutConfig) BurnoutResult {
47		result := BurnoutResult{
48			Warnings:  []BurnoutWarning{},
49			Available: true,
50		}
51	
52		// Check 1: Long work day (>10h screen-on)
53		if screen.Available {
54			longDayHours := screen.ScreenOnMinutes / 60
55			if longDayHours >= config.LongDayHours {
56				result.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, BurnoutWarning{
57					Type:        "long_day",
58					Message:     fmt.Sprintf("Long work day: %dh+ screen time", longDayHours),
59					Severity:    "medium",
60					MetricValue: longDayHours,
61				})
62			}
63		}
64	
65		// Open knowledgeC.db once for all DB-backed checks
66		db, err := openKnowledgeDB()
67		if err == nil {
68			defer db.Close()
69	
70			// Check 2: High app switching rate (>50 switches/hour)
71			appSwitchRate, err := calculateAppSwitchRate(ctx, db)
72			if err == nil && appSwitchRate > 0 {
73				if appSwitchRate >= config.AppSwitchesPerHour {
74					result.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, BurnoutWarning{
75						Type:        "high_switching",
76						Message:     fmt.Sprintf("High task switching: %d app switches/hour", appSwitchRate),
77						Severity:    "medium",
78						MetricValue: appSwitchRate,
79					})
80				}
81			}
82	
83			// Check 4: Late night work (activity past midnight)
84			lateNightMinutes, err := detectLateNightWork(ctx, db)
85			if err == nil && lateNightMinutes > 0 {
86				result.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, BurnoutWarning{
87					Type:        "late_night",
88					Message:     fmt.Sprintf("Late night work: %d minutes past midnight", lateNightMinutes),
89					Severity:    "high",
90					MetricValue: lateNightMinutes,
91				})
92			}
93	
94			// Check 5: No breaks (continuous focus >4h)
95			longestStreak, err := calculateLongestNoBreakPeriod(ctx, db)
96			if err == nil && longestStreak >= config.NoBreakHours*60 {
97				result.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, BurnoutWarning{
98					Type:        "no_breaks",
99					Message:     fmt.Sprintf("No breaks: %dh+ continuous focus", longestStreak/60),
100					Severity:    "high",
101					MetricValue: longestStreak / 60,
102				})
103			}
104		}
105	
106		// Check 3: Tab overload (>100 tabs)
107		if browsers.Available && browsers.TotalTabs >= config.MaxTabs {
108			result.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, BurnoutWarning{
109				Type:        "tab_overload",
110				Message:     fmt.Sprintf("Browser overload: %d open tabs", browsers.TotalTabs),
111				Severity:    "low",
112				MetricValue: browsers.TotalTabs,
113			})
114		}
115	
116		return result
117	}
118	
119	// calculateAppSwitchRate calculates the number of app switches per hour
120	func calculateAppSwitchRate(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (int, error) {
121		startTimestamp, endTimestamp := todayTimestampRange()
122	
123		// Count distinct app usage events (each represents a switch)
124		query := `
125			SELECT COUNT(*) as switch_count
126			FROM ZOBJECT
127			WHERE ZSTREAMNAME = '/app/usage'
128				AND ZSTARTDATE >= ?
129				AND ZENDDATE <= ?
130				AND ZVALUESTRING IS NOT NULL
131				AND ZVALUESTRING != ''
132		`
133	
134		var switchCount int
135		if err := db.QueryRowContext(ctx, query, startTimestamp, endTimestamp).Scan(&switchCount); err != nil {
136			return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to query switch count: %w", err)
137		}
138	
139		// Calculate rate per hour
140		now := time.Now()
141		midnight := time.Date(now.Year(), now.Month(), now.Day(), 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
142		hoursActive := time.Since(midnight).Hours()
143		if hoursActive < 1 {
144			hoursActive = 1
145		}
146	
147		rate := int(float64(switchCount) / hoursActive)
148		return rate, nil
149	}
150	
151	// detectLateNightWork detects app usage past midnight (00:00-06:00)
152	func detectLateNightWork(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (int, error) {
153		now := time.Now()
154		midnight := time.Date(now.Year(), now.Month(), now.Day(), 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
155		earlyMorning := midnight.Add(6 * time.Hour)
156	
157		startTimestamp := midnight.Sub(coreDataEpoch).Seconds()
158		endTimestamp := earlyMorning.Sub(coreDataEpoch).Seconds()
159	
160		// Sum up activity time in late night hours
161		query := `
162			SELECT SUM(ZENDDATE - ZSTARTDATE) as total_seconds
163			FROM ZOBJECT
164			WHERE ZSTREAMNAME = '/app/usage'
165				AND ZSTARTDATE >= ?
166				AND ZENDDATE <= ?
167				AND ZVALUESTRING IS NOT NULL
168				AND ZVALUESTRING != ''
169		`
170	
171		var totalSeconds sql.NullFloat64
172		if err := db.QueryRowContext(ctx, query, startTimestamp, endTimestamp).Scan(&totalSeconds); err != nil {
173			return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to query late night activity: %w", err)
174		}
175	
176		if !totalSeconds.Valid {
177			return 0, nil
178		}
179	
180		return int(totalSeconds.Float64 / 60), nil // Return minutes
181	}
182	
183	// calculateLongestNoBreakPeriod finds the longest continuous work period without breaks
184	func calculateLongestNoBreakPeriod(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (int, error) {
185		startTimestamp, endTimestamp := todayTimestampRange()
186	
187		// Get all app usage intervals ordered by time
188		query := `
189			SELECT ZSTARTDATE, ZENDDATE
190			FROM ZOBJECT
191			WHERE ZSTREAMNAME = '/app/usage'
192				AND ZSTARTDATE >= ?
193				AND ZENDDATE <= ?
194				AND ZVALUESTRING IS NOT NULL
195				AND ZVALUESTRING != ''
196			ORDER BY ZSTARTDATE ASC
197		`
198	
199		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, query, startTimestamp, endTimestamp)
200		if err != nil {
201			return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to query intervals: %w", err)
202		}
203		defer rows.Close()
204	
205		type interval struct {
206			start float64
207			end   float64
208		}
209	
210		var intervals []interval
211		for rows.Next() {
212			var start, end float64
213			if err := rows.Scan(&start, &end); err != nil {
214				continue
215			}
216			intervals = append(intervals, interval{start, end})
217		}
218	
219		if len(intervals) == 0 {
220			return 0, nil
221		}
222	
223		// Find longest continuous period (gaps < 15 minutes are considered continuous)
224		const maxGapMinutes = 15
225		maxPeriod := 0
226		currentPeriodStart := intervals[0].start
227		currentPeriodEnd := intervals[0].end
228	
229		for i := 1; i < len(intervals); i++ {
230			gap := int((intervals[i].start - currentPeriodEnd) / 60)
231	
232			if gap <= maxGapMinutes {
233				// Continue current period
234				currentPeriodEnd = intervals[i].end
235			} else {
236				// End current period, calculate duration
237				periodMinutes := int((currentPeriodEnd - currentPeriodStart) / 60)
238				if periodMinutes > maxPeriod {
239					maxPeriod = periodMinutes
240				}
241	
242				// Start new period
243				currentPeriodStart = intervals[i].start
244				currentPeriodEnd = intervals[i].end
245			}
246		}
247	
248		// Check final period
249		periodMinutes := int((currentPeriodEnd - currentPeriodStart) / 60)
250		if periodMinutes > maxPeriod {
251			maxPeriod = periodMinutes
252		}
253	
254		return maxPeriod, nil
255	}
